1	# Story 16 Final — Run-detail permalink hardening (post-codex)
2	
3	## Reframing per codex finding 1
4	Story 16 is **NOT a new product surface**. It is the permalink hardening of the `runs/[runId]` page that Story 8 already redirects to. Story 8 lands the list + inline expansion + redirect targets. Story 16 makes the **destination page lossless and shareable**. Anything beyond that is scope creep and gets dropped.
5	
6	## Diagnosis (unchanged)
7	- Tool calls already captured at drain time (`agent_runner_service.go`, `ToolCallEntry`) and exposed via `ExecutionToolCallConnection`.
8	- `executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte` (358 lines) has Manifest/Prompt/Result/Session/Tools tabs — proven pattern, will be promoted into `lib/runDetail/` and reused.
9	- `Run` resolver exposes only `promptSummary`, `evidenceLinks`, `outcome`, `baseRevision`, `resultRevision`. **Lossy.**
10	- `AgentSession` already has full `prompt`, `response`, `stderr`, `tokens{...}`, `billingMode`. Tool calls live on `Execution`, not `AgentSession`.
11	
12	## Relationship to Story 8 — SUBSET (sibling, sequenced after)
13	Story 8 sets the redirect target `/runs/[runId]?bundle=true` and inline expansion. Story 16 makes that destination URL render the lossless detail (full prompt/response/tool-call/evidence) so the redirect from `/executions/[id]` is not a regression. Without Story 16, Story 8 ships a regression because `/executions/[id]` (rich) collapses into `/runs/[runId]` (summary-only).
14	
15	## Decisions recorded (per codex BLOCKs)
16	
17	### Live vs post-hoc (codex 2)
18	**Recorded scope: completed-run audit + running-state linkage.** v1 does NOT include full live transcript replay, but the run-detail page MUST:
19	- Display run status (running / merged / preserved / failed) with last-update timestamp.
20	- For running runs, link to the originating worker page where existing `workerProgress` subscription already shows last-N events. No new subscription infra in this story.
21	- Plan explicitly states: "Story 16 is audit + supervision-link, not live replay." Live replay deferred to a later story.
22	
23	### Tool-call persistence (codex 3)
24	**Persist normalized `ToolCallEntry` at drain time directly into the run-layer detail backing**, not by parsing logs at resolver time. Reuses existing drain code in `service_run.go`. Log parsing remains only as a one-off backfill migration. Resolver always reads from the persisted normalized store.
25	
26	### Permission model (codex 4)
27	**Recorded rule, v1**: visibility of full prompt/response/tools/evidence on a run is gated by the same authorization that gates the parent project's artifact access. No new ACL surface. In coordinator+clients (Story 14), a coordinator that has artifact access to a remote client's project transitively has transcript access. Story 14 owns any future per-field ACL.
28	
29	### Redaction (codex 5)
30	**Recorded policy, v1**: no redaction. Trust boundary = project membership. Documented in FEAT-008 with explicit threat-boundary statement: "Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args. Operators must not store production secrets in repos consumed by DDx agents." Future redaction is a separate feature.
31	
32	### Evidence browser (codex 6) — DROPPED to minimum viable
33	v1 evidence tab is **list + metadata + download + raw small-text inline view only**. No Markdown rendering, no syntax highlighting, no hex preview. Whitelist: `manifest.json`, `prompt.md`, `result.json`, `*.txt`, `*.md`, files <64KB. Anything else: download link only.
34	
35	### Performance AC (codex 9) — DROPPED
36	Replaced with: lazy-load tabs (Tools and Evidence load on tab activation), pagination defaults (50 tool calls per page), and regression e2e tests at p95 dataset (200 tool calls, 30 evidence files) for "renders without crash, all tabs interactive within one render frame." No fake-precision millisecond budgets.
37	
38	## Spec changes
39	- **FEAT-008**: add Run-detail-page subsection. Document the trust boundary statement (no redaction, project membership = transcript access). Document evidence browser whitelist.
40	- **FEAT-010**: extend Run resolver contract to include `prompt`, `response`, `stderr`, `toolCalls(first, after)`, `bundleFiles[]`, `bundleFile(path)` with bundle-root confinement. Document normalized `ToolCallEntry` is the canonical store, populated at drain.
41	- **FEAT-019**: review surface points to `/runs/[runId]` deep page.
42	
43	## Implementation surface
44	
45	### Backend (one bead, codex 7)
46	- Persist `ToolCallEntry` into AgentSession/Run detail backing at drain time (extend existing drain in `service_run.go` / `agent_runner_service.go`).
47	- Extend `Run` resolver: `prompt`, `response`, `stderr`, `toolCalls(first, after)`, `bundleFiles[]` (path, size, mimeType), `bundleFile(path) -> {content, truncated, sizeBytes}`.
48	- Bundle-root confinement: canonical-path check; reject anything outside `<projectRoot>/.ddx/executions/<runId>/`.
49	- Whitelist enforcement for inline content: only allowed extensions/sizes return `content`; everything else returns `truncated=true, sizeBytes=N` and a download URL.
50	
51	### Frontend
52	- Promote shared subcomponents from `executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte` into `lib/runDetail/` (tab shell, prompt panel, response panel, tools panel, session panel).
53	- Rebuild `runs/[runId]/+page.svelte` as tabbed: **Overview / Prompt / Response / Tools / Evidence / Session**.
54	- Tab state in URL (`?tab=tools`); deep-link friendly.
55	- Overview: status, run/try/work layer chip, base/result rev, link to originating worker (for running state, codex 2).
56	- Evidence: list + metadata + download + inline view for whitelisted small text only.
57	
58	### Tests (split per codex 8)
59	- Backend bead: resolver unit tests (path traversal, size cap, whitelist), tool-call persistence at drain time.
60	- Page bead: e2e for tab switching + URL deep-link round-trip.
61	- Evidence bead: e2e for whitelist enforcement, large-file download path, missing-path 404.
62	- Final bead: spec docs + cross-cutting e2e (full prompt visible, full response visible, tool trace visible, evidence list visible).
63	
64	## Acceptance Criteria
65	1. `/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/runs/[runId]` renders tabbed detail: Overview, Prompt, Response, Tools, Evidence, Session.
66	2. Prompt tab shows full `prompt` body for both `layer=run` and `layer=try`.
67	3. Response tab shows full `response` (run-layer) or `result.json` body (try-layer) with verdict badge.
68	4. Tools tab shows paginated tool-call trace for both layers; data sourced from persisted `ToolCallEntry` (not log parsing); `Load more` paginates.
69	5. Evidence tab lists every file in the run's bundle with size + mimeType; whitelisted small text files (`*.txt`, `*.md`, `manifest.json`, `prompt.md`, `result.json`, <64 KB) render inline as raw text; everything else is download-only.
70	6. Session tab shows tokens (prompt/completion/total/cached), billingMode, base/result revs, stderr or stderrPath link.
71	7. Tab state reflected in URL query; refresh restores state.
72	8. For running runs, Overview shows status + a "View live progress" link to the originating worker page (no new subscription infra in this story).
73	9. Bundle-file resolver rejects any path outside `<projectRoot>/.ddx/executions/<runId>/` with 404 (canonical-path check; tested with `..`, symlink, absolute paths).
74	10. Tools and Evidence tabs lazy-load on activation; default page size 50 tool calls; large evidence file (>64 KB) shows size + download button only.
75	11. FEAT-008 records trust-boundary statement: project membership grants raw transcript visibility; no redaction in v1.
76	
77	## Risks
78	- Tool-call persistence at drain may need schema migration on AgentSession — addressed inside backend bead.
79	- Bundle-file path traversal — explicit test required.
80	- Story 8 must land 8.0 (data canonicalization) and 8.2 (row-expansion port) first; sequence Story 16 after.
81	
82	## Bead breakdown (codex-reconciled)
83	
84	| Bead | Title | Deps | Tier |
85	|---|---|---|---|
86	| 16.0 | **Backend: Run detail resolver + normalized tool-call persistence + bundle-file confinement.** Persist `ToolCallEntry` at drain time onto AgentSession/Run detail. Extend `Run` GraphQL with `prompt`, `response`, `stderr`, `toolCalls`, `bundleFiles`, `bundleFile`. Canonical-path bundle-root check. Resolver unit tests (path traversal, size cap, whitelist). | Story 8.0 | smart |
87	| 16.1 | **Frontend: rebuild `runs/[runId]/+page.svelte` as tabbed Overview/Prompt/Response/Tools/Session page.** Promote shared subcomponents into `lib/runDetail/`. URL-driven tab state. Page e2e (tab switching, deep-link round-trip). | 16.0, Story 8.2 | cheap-then-review |
88	| 16.2 | **Evidence tab: bundle-file list + metadata + download + whitelist inline raw-text view (≤64 KB, allowed extensions).** Evidence-specific e2e (whitelist enforcement, large-file download, missing-path 404). | 16.0, 16.1 | cheap |
89	| 16.3 | **Specs + cross-cutting e2e + telemetry.** Update FEAT-008 (run-detail subsection + trust-boundary), FEAT-010 (resolver contract), FEAT-019 (review surface link). End-to-end e2e: full prompt visible, full response visible, tool trace visible, evidence list visible. Audit event for "raw transcript viewed". | 16.1, 16.2 | cheap |
90	
91	4 beads. (Reduced from 5 by collapsing 16.0+16.1 per codex finding 7.)
92	
93	## Open questions resolved
94	- **Live tailing**: out of scope, link-to-worker only.
95	- **Redaction**: out of scope, trust boundary documented.
96	- **Permission scope**: equals project artifact access; Story 14 owns any future ACL.
97	- **AgentSession tool-call source**: persist at drain, not parse logs.

2	# Single source of truth for the SvelteKit UI.
3	# Every type field is doc-commented. Relay cursor connections on all lists.
4	
5	# ============================================
6	# Scalars
7	# ============================================
8	
9	"""ISO-8601 datetime string"""
10	scalar DateTime
11	
12	"""Arbitrary JSON blob (used for opaque map fields)"""
13	scalar JSON
14	
15	# ============================================
16	# Interfaces
17	# ============================================
18	
19	"""Node is the Relay global object identification interface"""
20	interface Node {
21	  """Globally unique identifier"""
22	  id: ID!
23	}
24	
25	# ============================================
26	# Pagination (Relay Cursor Connections)
27	# ============================================
28	
29	"""PageInfo carries cursor-based pagination state (Relay spec)"""
30	type PageInfo {
31	  """True when more edges exist after the last returned edge"""
32	  hasNextPage: Boolean!
33	  """True when more edges exist before the first returned edge"""
34	  hasPreviousPage: Boolean!
35	  """Cursor of the first edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
36	  startCursor: String
37	  """Cursor of the last edge in the current page (null when empty)"""
38	  endCursor: String
39	}
40

42	# Node & Project
43	# ============================================
44

46	type NodeInfo implements Node {

48	  id: ID!
49	  """Human-readable node name"""
50	  name: String!
51	  """Timestamp when the server process started"""

46	// agent service. It takes a sealed ResolvedConfig (durable knobs) and an
47	// AgentRunRuntime (per-invocation plumbing/intent), and is the SD-024
48	// successor to the retired RunViaService/RunViaServiceWith entry points.

51	// not the upstream service. These are not "carve-outs pending migration" —
52	// they are different products from upstream's same-named stubs. DDx's
53	// virtual is a content-addressed record/replay dictionary keyed by

55	// virtual.response into Metadata. DDx's script reads a filesystem directive
56	// file; upstream does not model this at all.
57	func RunWithConfigViaService(ctx context.Context, workDir string, rcfg config.ResolvedConfig, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {
58		if ctx == nil {
59			ctx = context.Background()
60		}
61	
62		harness := rcfg.Passthrough().Harness
63	
64		if harness == "virtual" || harness == "script" {
65			sessionLogDir := runtime.SessionLogDirOverride
66			if sessionLogDir == "" {

68			}

70			r := NewRunner(cfg)
71			r.WorkDir = workDir
72			return r.runInternal(RunArgs{

75				Prompt:        runtime.Prompt,

81				ModelRef:      rcfg.ModelRef(),
82				Effort:        rcfg.Effort(),
83				Timeout:       rcfg.Timeout(),
84				WallClock:     rcfg.WallClock(),
85				WorkDir:       runtime.WorkDir,
86				Permissions:   rcfg.Permissions(),
87				SessionLogDir: sessionLogDir,
88			})
89		}
90	
91		factory := serviceRunFactory
92		if factory == nil {

94		}
95		svc, err := factory(workDir)

97			return nil, fmt.Errorf("agent: build service: %w", err)
98		}
